title: Google Calendar OAuth2 connection refused error
symptom: After granting permissions during Google OAuth2 authentication, the user is redirected to a page showing 'This site can’t be reached localhost refused to connect. ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED'.
applies_when: Docker environment with incorrect webhook URL
not_this_if: Webhook URL is correctly set to a public domain
root_cause: The error occurs because the OAuth Redirect URL is set to 'localhost', which is not accessible from the internet. When the user tries to authenticate, Google redirects back to 'localhost', leading to a connection refusal.
fix_steps: Check the current webhook URL by running 'sudo docker exec -it [container_name] printenv'.
If the webhook URL is set to 'localhost', stop and remove the current n8n container using 'sudo docker stop [container_name]' and 'sudo docker rm n8n'.
Run a new n8n container with the correct webhook URL set to your domain using the provided docker run command.
agent_instruction: Update the webhook URL in your n8n Docker container to use your public domain instead of localhost.
